name: py-async-patterns
description: Async/await patterns for FastAPI and SQLAlchemy. Use when working with async code, database sessions, concurrent operations, or debugging async issues in Python.
capabilities: Guide AsyncSession lifecycle management and dependency injection
Optimize concurrent versus sequential database queries
Implement proper transaction boundaries with commit and rollback
Configure connection pool settings for asyncpg compatibility
Use BackgroundTasks for non-blocking post-response operations
Prevent deadlocks with consistent lock ordering
author: CJHarmath
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing markdown patterns and Python code examples. Contains no executable code, scripts, network calls, or file modifications. All external command references are in markdown code blocks showing grep/ruff commands for detection purposes only. The skill provides guidance through prompts and does not access or execute anything on user systems.
valueStatement: Async Python code often suffers from race conditions, session leaks, and connection pool issues. This skill provides battle-tested patterns for FastAPI and SQLAlchemy that prevent common async pitfalls.
limitations: Does not generate new async code from scratch
Does not execute or test async code
Does not modify existing codebase files
Does not integrate with async debugging tools

useCases.title: Fix Session Leaks
useCases.description: Identify and resolve async session management issues that cause stale data and connection exhaustion.

useCases.title: Optimize Query Performance
useCases.description: Transform sequential database queries into parallel operations using asyncio.gather for faster endpoints.

useCases.title: Implement Safe Transactions
useCases.description: Handle multi-step database operations with proper commit, rollback, and refresh patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Session Management
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me fix session lifecycle issues in my FastAPI app. I am seeing 'Session is closed' errors and connection timeouts.

promptTemplates.title: Query Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: I have multiple independent database queries in my endpoint that are running slowly. Show me how to use asyncio.gather to run them in parallel.

promptTemplates.title: Transaction Safety
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to implement a multi-step transfer operation between accounts. Show me the correct pattern for transactions with commit, rollback, and refresh.

promptTemplates.title: Background Operations
promptTemplates.prompt: My endpoint is slow because it sends emails and updates analytics after processing. Show me how to use BackgroundTasks or asyncio.create_task for non-blocking operations.

bestPractices: Always scope AsyncSession to individual requests using dependency injection
Use asyncio.gather for independent queries but sequential await for dependent queries
Acquire database locks in consistent order to prevent deadlocks in concurrent transfers
antiPatterns: Storing global session references that survive across requests
Running independent queries sequentially instead of using asyncio.gather
Forgetting to call session.refresh() after commit to get database-generated values

faq.question: Does this skill work with synchronous SQLAlchemy?
faq.answer: No. This skill covers async SQLAlchemy patterns with create_async_engine and AsyncSession. Synchronous patterns use different session management.

faq.question: What connection pool settings does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: For asyncpg, NullPool is recommended to avoid connection reuse issues. This creates new connections per request and closes them after.

faq.question: Can I use these patterns with other async frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes. The session lifecycle and transaction patterns apply to any async Python framework using SQLAlchemy async.

faq.question: Does this skill access my data or files?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides guidance through prompts. It does not read, write, or execute any code on your system.

faq.question: Why am I getting connection timeouts with my async code?
faq.answer: Timeouts usually indicate connection pool exhaustion from session leaks. Check that sessions are closed properly and not stored globally.

faq.question: How is this different from the official SQLAlchemy async documentation?
faq.answer: This skill provides battle-tested patterns specifically for FastAPI web applications including common pitfalls and detection commands.
